chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Next we will talk about our event object.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Whenever an event occurs, an event object is associated with it which is automatically passed to our handler.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: For example, this button click event, handle click.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: In this handle click, we can write an argument for ourselves which is called event.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: And if we want, we can print it. Let's print it after hello.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Here, console.log event.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: And this event object has all the information related to the event.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: It has a target, which element has been used for the event and all other details.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So we can print all those things here.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Let's save it and let's refresh it once.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So once we will go here and click on our button.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So when we clicked, hello was printed for us and our event object was printed for us.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Event object has a lot of different attributes and it has a lot of different methods.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Predefined methods.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: One of the most popular method is our prevent default which we use in the case of forms.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Now we will see how to use it.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: We can see the type of our event.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: If we want, we can see the target.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Target means for which element this event has occurred.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So target has all the information related to target.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So we can see that this event has appeared for button.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Screen X, Screen Y, we can see the position of our screen page and many other related details.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Now what we will do in this, we will try to design a new component which will be called form.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Now we will make a normal form in this.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: We will not go into much detail about forms because forms are a little complex topic in react which we will cover in detail later.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So for now, we are going to create a simple form.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Let's write export default function and this will be our form component.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Form component is a simple form which returns.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: We can use our form tag in this.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: We have an input which we can define.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Along with this, there is a submit button which we can write.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: We can call it submit.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: In fact, we don't need much input but for now, let's define an input.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Let's put placeholder in this.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So this form has been created for us.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Now along with form, let's suppose as soon as our form is submitted, what will be that event?

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: That will be our on submit event.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: For forms, there is a submit event.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: As soon as the form is submitted, we can call its handler for it.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So for this, let's create a function.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: We will call it handle.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: We can call it form submit or handle submit.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: And here we want to print console.log

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: form was submitted.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So let's save this and along with this, submit.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Okay, this should not be submit. This should be a button.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: And in on click, we add our method which is handle form submit.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Let's save this.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: And let's go to app.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: First, we have to import our form.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: And then we will display this form.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So our form is here for us.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Let's refresh to clear the console.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So DOM gets updated but console window doesn't get updated.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So what does React do? DOM elements.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: That means React's job is to keep UI elements updated.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So it updates UI not console window.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So if you want console clear, you have to refresh for that.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Now here, we can even submit the form without writing something

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: or we can write abcd and submit the form.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: As soon as we submit the form, you will notice one thing.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Suddenly something was displayed here and then it disappeared.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: That means everything got refreshed.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: If we click on submit button again,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: I don't know if you are catching it in the screen recording or not.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: But as soon as it was submitted,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: the console.log form was printed there and submitted.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: But that thing is not persisting.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: That thing is not stopping on the screen.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So this is the default behavior of the form.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: As soon as it is submitted in the form,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: by default, this thing that is being printed,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: it appears for a while and then disappears.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So if we want to prevent this default behavior,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: then what we can do is,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: our event object has a special method inside it

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: which is called prevent default.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: And what does prevent default method do?

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: It prevents all the default behaviors associated with our event object.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So here, if I call this method event.preventDefault

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: and if we save it,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: and let's refresh it once.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So if we submit our form this time,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: then our default behavior will be prevented

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: and the thing that will be for us will persist.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: If we submit the form again,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: the thing will persist again,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: persist again, and persist again.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: One more thing you will notice is that

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: if it was our default behavior,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: we will save and refresh it.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: If I write A, B, C, D and I submit,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: then what happens in that case is,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: because of default behavior, the input also gets empty.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: But because this time we have completely prevented the default behavior,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: and if I now write something and submit,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: then what happens is,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: our input element is not yet empty.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So generally, a lot of default behaviors

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: associated with the form based on submission,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: if we want to prevent all those behaviors,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: then we can access the event object to prevent those behaviors.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: This fact, this special thing,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: we will be using it later while reading the forms.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: But for now, it is important to know.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: One thing here is that we did not write our onSubmit event,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: we wrote onClick event.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Rest, no change will come in the functionality.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Because if any button is present in any form,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: then by default, what does the form do?

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: It treats it like a submit button and it gets submitted.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Rest, if we want to check the submit functionality specifically,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: then we will write our event in the form,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: because this event is for our form,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: we will call it onSubmit.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: And in onSubmit, we will handle the form submit.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: If we save,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: if we comment out the prevent default,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: refresh, if I write something here called ABCD,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: and as soon as I submit it,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: something gets printed and disappears.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: But as soon as we do prevent default for our event,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: refresh, ABCD, submit,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: so this time our form is being submitted for us,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: and things are being printed here.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: So default behavior,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: whether onClick is applied here,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: or whether onSubmit is applied here,

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: it will be in both cases.

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: Because by default, all the buttons of the form

chunks.title: Event Object.mp3
chunks.text: are submitting things.

text: Next we will talk about our event object. Whenever an event occurs, an event object is associated with it which is automatically passed to our handler. For example, this button click event, handle click. In this handle click, we can write an argument for ourselves which is called event. And if we want, we can print it. Let's print it after hello. Here, console.log event. And this event object has all the information related to the event. It has a target, which element has been used for the event and all other details. So we can print all those things here. Let's save it and let's refresh it once. So once we will go here and click on our button. So when we clicked, hello was printed for us and our event object was printed for us. Event object has a lot of different attributes and it has a lot of different methods. Predefined methods. One of the most popular method is our prevent default which we use in the case of forms. Now we will see how to use it. We can see the type of our event. If we want, we can see the target. Target means for which element this event has occurred. So target has all the information related to target. So we can see that this event has appeared for button. Screen X, Screen Y, we can see the position of our screen page and many other related details. Now what we will do in this, we will try to design a new component which will be called form. Now we will make a normal form in this. We will not go into much detail about forms because forms are a little complex topic in react which we will cover in detail later. So for now, we are going to create a simple form. Let's write export default function and this will be our form component. Form component is a simple form which returns. We can use our form tag in this. We have an input which we can define. Along with this, there is a submit button which we can write. We can call it submit. In fact, we don't need much input but for now, let's define an input. Let's put placeholder in this. So this form has been created for us. Now along with form, let's suppose as soon as our form is submitted, what will be that event? That will be our on submit event. For forms, there is a submit event. As soon as the form is submitted, we can call its handler for it. So for this, let's create a function. We will call it handle. We can call it form submit or handle submit. And here we want to print console.log form was submitted. So let's save this and along with this, submit. Okay, this should not be submit. This should be a button. And in on click, we add our method which is handle form submit. Let's save this. And let's go to app. First, we have to import our form. And then we will display this form. So our form is here for us. Let's refresh to clear the console. So DOM gets updated but console window doesn't get updated. So what does React do? DOM elements. That means React's job is to keep UI elements updated. So it updates UI not console window. So if you want console clear, you have to refresh for that. Now here, we can even submit the form without writing something or we can write abcd and submit the form. As soon as we submit the form, you will notice one thing. Suddenly something was displayed here and then it disappeared. That means everything got refreshed. If we click on submit button again, I don't know if you are catching it in the screen recording or not. But as soon as it was submitted, the console.log form was printed there and submitted. But that thing is not persisting. That thing is not stopping on the screen. So this is the default behavior of the form. As soon as it is submitted in the form, by default, this thing that is being printed, it appears for a while and then disappears. So if we want to prevent this default behavior, then what we can do is, our event object has a special method inside it which is called prevent default. And what does prevent default method do? It prevents all the default behaviors associated with our event object. So here, if I call this method event.preventDefault and if we save it, and let's refresh it once. So if we submit our form this time, then our default behavior will be prevented and the thing that will be for us will persist. If we submit the form again, the thing will persist again, persist again, and persist again. One more thing you will notice is that if it was our default behavior, we will save and refresh it. If I write A, B, C, D and I submit, then what happens in that case is, because of default behavior, the input also gets empty. But because this time we have completely prevented the default behavior, and if I now write something and submit, then what happens is, our input element is not yet empty. So generally, a lot of default behaviors associated with the form based on submission, if we want to prevent all those behaviors, then we can access the event object to prevent those behaviors. This fact, this special thing, we will be using it later while reading the forms. But for now, it is important to know. One thing here is that we did not write our onSubmit event, we wrote onClick event. Rest, no change will come in the functionality. Because if any button is present in any form, then by default, what does the form do? It treats it like a submit button and it gets submitted. Rest, if we want to check the submit functionality specifically, then we will write our event in the form, because this event is for our form, we will call it onSubmit. And in onSubmit, we will handle the form submit. If we save, if we comment out the prevent default, refresh, if I write something here called ABCD, and as soon as I submit it, something gets printed and disappears. But as soon as we do prevent default for our event, refresh, ABCD, submit, so this time our form is being submitted for us, and things are being printed here. So default behavior, whether onClick is applied here, or whether onSubmit is applied here, it will be in both cases. Because by default, all the buttons of the form are submitting things.
